preamble: The mapping of the voiced dental fricative [ð] to a retroflex stop [ɖ] is a systematic adaptation in languages that lack dental fricatives but have a robust retroflex series, such as the languages of South Asia and some Scandinavian dialects.

examples.note: Common mapping in South Asian languages which lack dental fricatives.
In dialects where dental fricatives merge with rhotic sounds to form retroflexes (e.g., 'fordi').
